project.name: Module 16b: Folder Locking
project.description: Per-folder password protection as a UI-level authorization gate. Each folder can optionally have its own password (independent of the vault password). The entire tree is already encrypted at rest by the vault password — folder locking adds a SECOND authorization layer: you need the vault password to decrypt the tree, then each locked folder's password to view its contents. Locked folders show a lock icon; contents hidden until folder password entered. Unlocked state is session-only (re-locked when popup closes or session locks). Free tier feature — no ProGate required.
project.parent_plan: Hush Consolidated Build Plan — Day 5 (folder locking)
project.critical_path_position: Module 16b is independent of 16a (ProGate) since folder locking is Free tier. Depends on Modules 1-13 (all complete). Can run in any order relative to 14, 15a, 16a.

user_stories.title: lib/folder-lock.ts — folder password hashing and verification
user_stories.description: Create lib/folder-lock.ts with: createFolderLock(password) → FolderLock { salt, hash } using Web Crypto SHA-256 with random salt. verifyFolderLock(password, lock) → boolean. Extend Folder type in lib/types.ts with optional lock?: FolderLock field. Pure lib/ module, zero React imports. FolderLock hashes are stored in the Folder metadata within the encrypted tree — protected by vault encryption at rest.
steps: Extend Folder type in lib/types.ts: add lock?: FolderLock. Define FolderLock = { readonly salt: string; readonly hash: string } (base64 strings). Default: undefined (not locked). IMPORTANT: this is an additive, optional change — existing Folder objects without lock are valid.
RED: Test: 'createFolderLock returns salt and hash'
RED: Test: 'createFolderLock generates unique salt each time'
RED: Test: 'verifyFolderLock returns true for correct password'
RED: Test: 'verifyFolderLock returns false for wrong password'
RED: Test: 'FolderLock salt is 16 bytes (base64 decoded)'
RED: Test: 'folder-lock.ts has zero React imports'

GREEN: Create lib/folder-lock.ts. Use crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', ...) with salt prepended. Salt: crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(16)). Hash: SHA-256(salt + utf8encode(password)). Both stored as base64.
REFACTOR: Verify lib/ purity. Update scaffold-smoke LIB_MODULES count. Verify existing Folder-using tests still pass (additive optional field).
acceptance_criteria: FolderLock type: { readonly salt: string; readonly hash: string }
Folder type extended with lock?: FolderLock (optional, additive — undefined means not locked)
createFolderLock: random 16-byte salt, SHA-256 hash, both base64 encoded
verifyFolderLock: recomputes hash with stored salt, compares
Unique salt per createFolderLock call (CSPRNG)
Existing tests pass without modification (additive type change)
Zero React imports in lib/folder-lock.ts
LIB_MODULES updated in scaffold-smoke
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/lib/folder-lock.test.ts && npx vitest run && npx tsc --noEmit

user_stories.title: Background handlers for SET_FOLDER_LOCK, REMOVE_FOLDER_LOCK, VERIFY_FOLDER_LOCK
user_stories.description: Add three new message types to the background service worker. SET_FOLDER_LOCK: takes folderPath + password → createFolderLock → update folder in tree → save. REMOVE_FOLDER_LOCK: takes folderPath + password → verifyFolderLock → remove lock → save. VERIFY_FOLDER_LOCK: takes folderPath + password → verifyFolderLock → return success/failure (no tree mutation — unlock state is UI-only).
steps: Add to lib/background-types.ts: SetFolderLockMessage { type, folderPath: readonly number[], password: string }, RemoveFolderLockMessage { type, folderPath: readonly number[], password: string }, VerifyFolderLockMessage { type, folderPath: readonly number[], password: string }. Add all three to BackgroundMessage union.
RED: Test: 'SET_FOLDER_LOCK creates lock on folder and saves tree'
RED: Test: 'SET_FOLDER_LOCK requires unlocked session'
RED: Test: 'REMOVE_FOLDER_LOCK verifies password before removing lock'
RED: Test: 'REMOVE_FOLDER_LOCK rejects wrong password'
RED: Test: 'VERIFY_FOLDER_LOCK returns success for correct password'
RED: Test: 'VERIFY_FOLDER_LOCK returns failure for wrong password'

GREEN: Wire handlers in entrypoints/background/handlers.ts. SET_FOLDER_LOCK: get folder by path → createFolderLock → set folder.lock → save tree. REMOVE_FOLDER_LOCK: get folder → verifyFolderLock → set folder.lock = undefined → save. VERIFY_FOLDER_LOCK: get folder → verifyFolderLock → return boolean (no tree mutation). Update switch in entrypoints/background/index.ts.
REFACTOR: Ensure getFolderByPath from lib/data-model.ts is used for path resolution. Verify exhaustive switch compiles.
acceptance_criteria: 3 new message types: SET_FOLDER_LOCK, REMOVE_FOLDER_LOCK, VERIFY_FOLDER_LOCK
SET_FOLDER_LOCK: creates lock hash, updates tree immutably, saves
REMOVE_FOLDER_LOCK: verifies password first, then removes lock (sets undefined)
VERIFY_FOLDER_LOCK: pure check, no tree mutation
All require unlocked session
Wrong password returns clear error with appropriate code (not crypto internals)
Exhaustive switch compiles
user_stories.verification: npx vitest run tests/unit/entrypoints/background.test.ts && npx tsc --noEmit && npx wxt build

user_stories.title: Folder lock UI — hook, dialogs, and tree integration
user_stories.description: Add lock/unlock visuals and dialogs to FolderItem. Locked folders show lock icon and hide children until password entered. Session-level unlock tracking via useFolderLockState hook (re-locked on popup close/session lock). Lock/Unlock/Remove Lock options in folder context menu. SetFolderLockDialog and VerifyFolderLockDialog shared components. Extends existing DialogState and ItemAction types.
steps: Create hooks/useFolderLockState.ts: manages Set<string> of unlocked folder paths. Path serialization: path.join('/') (e.g., [0,2,1] → '0/2/1'). Exposes: isUnlocked(path: readonly number[]) → boolean, markUnlocked(path: readonly number[]) → void, clearAll() → void. Clears on session lock events (listen for LOCK message response or session state change). Uses useReducer internally.
Extend ItemAction type: add 'set-folder-lock' | 'remove-folder-lock' variants with path and folder fields.

Extend DialogState discriminated union in TreeScreen: add { type: 'set-folder-lock'; path: readonly number[]; folder: Folder } and { type: 'verify-folder-lock'; path: readonly number[]; folder: Folder } variants.
Create components/shared/SetFolderLockDialog.tsx: password + confirm password inputs, sends SET_FOLDER_LOCK via useSendMessage.
Create components/shared/VerifyFolderLockDialog.tsx: single password input, sends VERIFY_FOLDER_LOCK, calls markUnlocked on success.

Extend FolderItem: if folder.lock exists, show lock icon (lucide-react Lock). If locked (not in unlocked set via isUnlocked), hide children and show 'Enter password to view'. Click opens VerifyFolderLockDialog.
Extend folder context menu: 'Lock Folder' (when folder.lock is undefined), 'Remove Lock' (when folder.lock exists, requires password via REMOVE_FOLDER_LOCK).
Wire dialogs into TreeScreen and ManagerApp using existing dialog state pattern.
RED: Test: 'useFolderLockState — isUnlocked returns false by default'
RED: Test: 'useFolderLockState — markUnlocked makes isUnlocked return true'
RED: Test: 'useFolderLockState — clearAll resets all unlocked paths'
RED: Test: 'locked folder hides children'
RED: Test: 'entering correct password reveals children'
RED: Test: 'wrong password shows error'
RED: Test: 'Lock Folder option opens SetFolderLockDialog'
RED: Test: 'Remove Lock verifies password before removing'
GREEN: Implement all components, hook, and wire into existing tree rendering.
acceptance_criteria: useFolderLockState hook: isUnlocked, markUnlocked, clearAll. Path serialized via path.join('/')
Hook clears unlocked state on session lock
Locked folder shows lock icon (lucide-react Lock)
Locked folder hides children until password entered
VerifyFolderLockDialog: password input + verify button + error display
SetFolderLockDialog: password + confirm + create button
Correct password calls markUnlocked → children visible
Wrong password shows error, input stays
Context menu: 'Lock Folder' (when no lock) / 'Remove Lock' (when locked)
DialogState extended with set-folder-lock and verify-folder-lock variants
ItemAction extended with set-folder-lock and remove-folder-lock variants
Works in both popup and manager (shared components + hook)
Zero business logic in components (all via messages to background)

RED: E2E: 'locked folder hides children'
RED: E2E: 'enter correct password → children visible'

Run full verification: tsc + eslint + vitest + wxt build + playwright

E2E: folder re-locks on session lock
Full verification passes
Zero regressions

what_exists.note: Read actual state from most recent progress.md. Module 16b can run after Modules 1-13 and is independent of 14, 15a, 16a.
what_exists.data_model: lib/data-model.ts: getFolderByPath() exists (Module 13). Folder type has type, id, name, children, dateAdded.
what_exists.folder_item: components/shared/FolderItem.tsx: Accordion-based folder with context menu (Edit/Move/Delete). Uses ItemAction discriminated union.
what_exists.tree_screen: components/screens/TreeScreen.tsx: DialogState discriminated union manages all dialogs. Pattern: action triggers dialog → dialog sends message → handler updates tree → dialog closes.

what_does_not_exist.folder_lock: No lib/folder-lock.ts. No FolderLock type. No lock property on Folder type.
what_does_not_exist.lock_messages: No SET_FOLDER_LOCK, REMOVE_FOLDER_LOCK, VERIFY_FOLDER_LOCK message types.
what_does_not_exist.lock_ui: No lock icon on folders. No lock/unlock dialogs. No useFolderLockState hook.

tech_stack.crypto: Web Crypto API for SHA-256 hashing (folder lock passwords). NOT using full PBKDF2 — folder lock is a UI authorization gate, not crypto-level encryption. The tree is already encrypted by vault password.
tech_stack.security_model: Two-layer security: (1) Vault password encrypts the entire tree with AES-256-GCM/PBKDF2 (lib/crypto.ts). (2) Folder lock adds UI-level authorization gate — contents hidden until folder password verified. Folder lock hashes live inside the encrypted tree (protected at rest).
tech_stack.session_unlock_tracking: Unlocked folders tracked in useFolderLockState hook via Set<string>. Path serialization: path.join('/') (e.g., [0,2,1] → '0/2/1'). NOT persisted — re-locked when popup closes, session locks, or browser restarts. This is intentional security behavior.
tech_stack.testing: Vitest + happy-dom for unit. Playwright for E2E.
forbidden_patterns: Persisting unlocked folder state to storage (session-only)
Using PBKDF2 for folder lock (overkill — SHA-256 with salt is sufficient for UI gate)
Mutating tree in place (all operations must be immutable)

read_first: components/shared/FolderItem.tsx (existing folder rendering, ItemAction, context menu)

inputs.what_it_provides: Folder type to extend with lock? field
Existing folder component, ItemAction type, context menu
Message transport hook for background communication

known_risks.issue: Extending Folder type may break existing tests
known_risks.action: lock field is optional (lock?: FolderLock). Existing Folder objects without lock are valid — undefined is the default. Run full test suite after type change.

known_risks.issue: SHA-256 without iterations is weaker than PBKDF2
known_risks.action: Acceptable. Folder lock is a UI gate, not crypto-level encryption. The tree itself is protected by AES-256-GCM with 600K PBKDF2 iterations. Folder lock prevents casual viewing, not cryptographic attack.
Track in useFolderLockState hook with useReducer (survives re-renders). Hook instance per entrypoint (popup/manager) — each has independent unlock state, which is correct.

metadata.session_protocol: One Claude Code session. Commit per story.
metadata.quality_gate: All tests pass. tsc clean. wxt build. Folder type change doesn't break existing tests. Lock state session-only. Zero plaintext passwords stored.
metadata.human_review_required: Verify: lock icon visible on locked folders, children hidden, session-only unlock, folder type change backward compatible.

execution_rules.clarifying_questions: Encouraged. Key: Folder type extension backward compatibility, SHA-256 vs PBKDF2 for folder lock, session unlock tracking approach, DialogState/ItemAction extension.
